metadata.title: How to Make Explainer Videos for Your Research
metadata.audience: Humanitarians AI Fellows — researchers learning the Brutalist pipeline
metadata.greeting_note: Merhaba = Turkish for Hello. Liam two-word budget.
metadata.note: COLD OPEN LAW: B00 = ClaudeComposerAsk. ILLUSTRATE LAW: inner beats = concept figures (Figs 1-6). HANDOFF LAW: B08 = Your Turn composer. OUTRO LAW: B09 = ClaudeTitleOutro restating title. Sign-in: 'Liam, for Humanitarians AI.' (not 'Liam, in for Bear' — this is HAI-branded). folderLabel @HumanitariansAI on every beat.

beats.narration_text: You did the research this week. Here's how to turn it into a video the world can actually watch — using Claude and the Brutalist system — in one sitting. Liam, for Humanitarians AI.

props.command: how do I turn my research into an explainer video — in one sitting?
props.segment: How to Make Explainer Videos for Your Research
output: Step 1: put your week in a folder
Step 2-3: get the Brutalist system + open Claude Code
Step 4: give Claude your specific prompt
Step 5: watch, then ask for changes

beats.narration_text: Six steps, one sitting. Your week's folder goes in. The Brutalist system provides the tools. Claude Code builds the video. You watch and revise. Then you publish. That's the whole pipeline — and every step happens today.

Step one: put your week in a folder. Notes, code, figures, data, the paper draft, screenshots — all of it. That folder is the raw material. Claude reads it to find the stories worth animating. One folder equals one conversation with the pipeline.

Step two: get the Brutalist system. Email hr@humanitarians.ai and you'll receive the toolkit and setup help. Step three: open Claude Code in the folder that holds both your work and the Brutalist skills. The command is: caffeinate claude --dangerously-skip-permissions. Caffeinate keeps your Mac awake through the render. The flag lets Claude run the whole pipeline without stopping for each permission.

Step four: give Claude the prompt — with your specifics. Start from the generic seed: create a claude-explainer in the Liam voice for every video idea in this folder. Then steer it. Name your project. Name the result. Name the figure worth showing. Name the one idea you want a viewer to leave with. The specifics are what make it yours. And if you're using the Liam voice, tell Claude to have Liam say: Liam, for your name and Humanitarians AI. Make it yours out loud.

props.sparkLine: The specifics are what make it yours.

beats.narration_text: Step five: watch, then ask for changes. Claude builds the video. Watch it. Anything off — a wrong number, a pace that drags, a chart that should be a different chart — just tell Claude in plain language. Plain language in, better video out. Iterate until it's right.

props.sparkLine: Plain language in, better video out.

beats.narration_text: Step six: publish to the channel. When it's ready, use Gaurav's publishing script — get it from hr@humanitarians.ai — to post your video to youtube.com/@humanitariansai, in the Fellows Research playlist. That's where your research explainer lives.

Let's recap with Claude. Six steps to turn your research week into a published video. Your folder of notes, code, and figures. The Brutalist system from the email. Claude Code in that folder with a specific prompt. Watch and revise. Then publish to the Fellows Research playlist.

artifactLines: Put your week's work in one folder — that's the raw material.
Email hr@humanitarians.ai for the Brutalist system and setup help.
Give Claude your specific prompt — name your project, result, and key idea.
Watch the video, then ask for changes in plain language.
Publish to youtube.com/@humanitariansai → Fellows Research playlist.

beats.narration_text: Here's the prompt to run in your own session. Here's my folder of this week's research — describe what you did. Create a claude-explainer in the Liam voice for every video idea in it, with animated visualizations and infographics, and have Liam say: Liam, for my name and Humanitarians AI. Fill in that bracket with one line about your own work. That's all Claude needs to start building.

props.command: Here's my folder of this week's research — [one line on what you did]. Create a claude-explainer in the Liam voice for every video idea in it, with animated visualizations and infographics, and have Liam say 'Liam, for [my name] and Humanitarians AI.'
props.segment: How to Make Explainer Videos for Your Research

beats.narration_text: How to Make Explainer Videos for Your Research. Liam, for Humanitarians AI.
